merged_events.event_description: United States opened consular relations with Laos by assigning a Vice Consul at Vientiane (the capital).

evidence.quote: The United States opened consular relations with Laos with the assignment of Charles J. Baker as Vice Consul at Vientiane on April 25, 1950.

merged_events.event_description: Diplomatic relations established when nonresident American Envoy Extraordinary and Minister Plenipotentiary Donald R. Heath presented credentials to the King of Laos; Heath was resident at Saigon.

evidence.quote: Diplomatic relations were established on July 29, 1950, when American Envoy Extraordinary and Minister Plenipotentiary Donald R. Heath presented his credentials to the King of Laos. Heath was also accredited to Cambodia and Viet-Nam and was resident at Saigon.

merged_events.event_description: The American Legation in Vientiane was established and opened under Chargé d'Affaires ad interim Paul L. Guest.

evidence.quote: The American Legation in Vientiane was established on August 22, 1950, when it opened under Chargé d'Affaires ad interim Paul L. Guest.

merged_events.event_description: The United States elevated its diplomatic mission in Vientiane from a Legation to an Embassy, per a joint announcement by the U.S. and Laotian governments.

evidence.quote: According to a joint announcement by the Governments of the United States and Laos on August 10, 1955, the United States elevated its diplomatic mission in Vientiane from a Legation to an Embassy.

merged_events.event_description: Following the proclamation of the Lao People's Democratic Republic, the U.S. downgraded its chief of mission from Ambassador to Chargé d'Affaires ad interim, reducing the mission below embassy level.

evidence.quote: After the proclamation of the founding of the Lao People's Democratic Republic (LPDR) in December 1975, the United States downgraded its chief of mission in Vientiane from Ambassador to Chargé d'Affaires ad interim.

merged_events.event_description: Ambassadorial relations restored when Ambassador Charles B. Salmon Jr. presented his credentials to the Lao Government.

evidence.quote: The United States posted its first Ambassador to the LPDR when Ambassador Charles B. Salmon Jr. presented his credentials to the Lao Government on August 6, 1992.

merged_events.event_description: Mission elevated from Legation to Embassy when Yost was promoted from Envoy Extraordinary and Minister Plenipotentiary to Ambassador Extraordinary and Plenipotentiary.

evidence.quote: Charles Woodruff Yost, Envoy Extraordinary and Minister Plenipotentiary. Appointed: 1954-08-18. Credentials presented: 1954-11-01. Promoted to Ambassador Extraordinary and Plenipotentiary 1955-08-19.

matched.notes: Exact match on date (1950-04-25) and status (Consulate). RDCR confirms opening of consular relations at Vientiane.
Exact match on date (1950-07-29) and status (Envoy Nonresident). RDCR confirms Heath presented credentials as nonresident envoy, resident at Saigon.
Exact match on date (1950-08-22) and status (Legation). RDCR confirms establishment of American Legation in Vientiane.

CSV date (1955-08-10) matches RDCR Event 3 (joint announcement of elevation). POCOM Event 6 gives 1955-08-19 (Yost's promotion date), which is a personnel event. Per source priority, RDCR's institutional date wins. CSV is correct.

missing_from_csv.event_description: Following the proclamation of the Lao People's Democratic Republic in December 1975, the U.S. downgraded its chief of mission from Ambassador to Chargé d'Affaires ad interim, effectively reducing the mission below full embassy level.
missing_from_csv.notes: Medium confidence. RDCR is clear about the downgrade but provides only a month-level date (December 1975). The exact status label ('Liaison' vs something else) could be debated, but the mission was maintained at a reduced level with a Chargé d'Affaires. Strong candidate for addition to CSV.

missing_from_csv.event_description: Ambassadorial relations restored when Ambassador Charles B. Salmon Jr. presented his credentials to the Lao Government on August 6, 1992, becoming the first U.S. Ambassador to the LPDR.
missing_from_csv.notes: High confidence. RDCR explicitly states this was the first Ambassador posted to the LPDR. This represents the restoration of full Embassy-level relations after the 1975 downgrade. Strong candidate for addition to CSV.
